def: adj. (f. -ī), Shaven.
s.m. Shaving the head; having the head shaved, tonsure; — baldness.

adj. & s.m. Silent, taciturn, reserved, not speaking; — a silent or reserved person; — a class of Hindū ascetics who make a vow to observe perpetual silence; — one who has overcome his passions and retired from the world; a holy sage (i.q. muni), a hermit, an ascetic.
s.f. A jar; — a small round basket; — a work-basket.
more: n. m. 1. A silent person. 2. A sect of jogis .
s. f. A small round basket, a workbasket: see مونا maunā. ¶ monī, s. f. Point, end.
adj. 1. Silent, tacit. 2. s. m. A class of mendicants who observe perpetual silence.

def: adj. Made of munja grass or its fibres.
s.f. A Brāhmaṇ's girdle or cord (made of a triple string of murja grass; — syn. mekhalā; janeʼū)
subs: s.m. The binding on of the munja-grass girdle; investiture with the distinguishing Brāhmaṇical thread.

def: adj. = mauṅḍā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Shaven.
s.f. = موسي mausī, q.v. s.f. Mother's sister, aunt.

more: n. m. S. मौलि, मुकुल a bud. Pr. maūlo . Blossoms, esp. mango blossoms.
E. n. m. S. मौल or मुकुट A crown worn by a bridegroom in the marriage procession. mauṛ, mukaṭ, tāj, kalg/?/ī , ( Bot .) Coma or tuft. mauṛ'ī , n. f. A crown of paper or date leaves worn by a bride.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = موڙ mauṛ, q.v. s.m. A bud; blossom.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

def: s.m. (dialec.) The shoulder.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

def: s.m. A crown, or a kind of high-crowned hat (such as is worn by a bridegroom at the time of marriage); a crest; a tuft; (in Bot.) coma, or tuft (syn. mukuṭ).
s.m. = mauṛ, q.v. s.m. A bud; blossom.

ety: maurā˚ = Prk. मोल्लाव(इ) or मोल्लावे(इ), fr. S. मौल+आपि; — or maur, q.v. +ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. (dialec.) To blossom; to flourish (i.q. maulnā).
more: v. n. To blossom (as a mango tree).
(from मौलि ) v. n. To blossom (as, the mango tree). See مولنا maulnā.

ety: maurā˚ = Prk. मोल्लाव(इ) or मोल्लावे(इ), fr. S. मौल+आपि; — or maur, q.v. +ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. (dialec.) To blossom; to flourish (i.q. maulnā).
s.m. corr. of mauṛ, q.v. s.m. A bud; blossom.
s.m. = mauḍhya, q.v. s.m. Stupidity, want of understanding, ignorance, folly, fatuity; — spiritual folly.

ety: n. of place fr. ورد 'to be present; to descend, alight,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of arrival, or of descending, or alighting, or appearing, or halting; — a place through which one passes; access, entrance, passage, approach; — station, quarters; — object sought.
more: (noun of place of ورد Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. The place of arrival, of descent, of alighting, of halting or appearing; station, object sought.

def: s.m. Stupidity, folly, fatuity.
v.n. (dialec.) = maulnā, q.v. v.n. To blossom, bud, bloom; to flourish; — v.t. To exhilarate; to intoxicate (said of bhang).
s.m. (rustic or dialec.) = maur = mauṛ, q.v. s.m. A bud; blossom.

ety: pass. part. of ورث 'to receive by inheritance'

more: (part. pass. of ورث Heb. ) adj. Hereditary; heritage.

def: adj. Inherited; hereditary; patrimonial; ancestral.
s.m. Mother's sister's husband, uncle.
more: s. m. An uncle, motherʼs sisterʼs husband.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to, or related to, a mother's sister
more: adj. Belonging to māosā , q. v. mauserā bhāī , n. m. A cousin (mother's sister's son). mauserī bahan , n. f. A cousin (mother's sister's daughter).
adj. mas. Belonging or related to the motherʼs sisterʼs husband; as, موسيرا بهاٴي mauserā bhāʼī, A cousin, son of a motherʼs sister. موسيري بـﮩن mauserī bahin, Daughter of a motherʼs sister.

def: s.f. Mother's sister, aunt.
more: s. f. An aunt, motherʼs sister.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُوسَى (mūsā), from Biblical Hebrew.

ety: n. of place fr. وصل 'to join'
def: s.m. (orig.) 'The place where two ropes (or other things) are joined or tied,' 'a junction'; — name of a city and district which joins Mesopotamia to Babylonia (situated on the western bank of the Tigris, opposite to the spot where Nineveh was supposed to have stood).
adj. Of or from Mausil; belonging to, or produced in, Mausil.

ety: n. of time and place fr. وسم 'to describe; to excel in beauty,' &c.
def: s.m. Time, or season (for anything)
more: n. m. Season time ( rut , 1). mausam-i-bahār , n. m. The season of spring. mausim-i-khizāṅ , Autumn. be-mausam , adj. See be-rut kā .
(noun of place or time from وسم Heb. ) s. m. Season, time. موسم بـﮩاري mausim-i-bahār-ī, The season of spring.
subs: or mausim-ě-bahārī, s.m. The season of spring
adj. Unseasonable; out of season (early, or late).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَوْسِم (mawsim), from Arabic مَوْسِم (mawsim).

def: adj. Of the season; seasonable; in season.
s.f. 1˚ corr. of mūsrī, q.v.; — 2˚ = maulsirī, q.v. s.f. (dim.) A small rat, or mouse.

part. adj. Described; qualified; distinguished, particularized, marked out; characterized (by); — endowed; — named; praised, celebrated; — commendable, laudable; — before-mentioned, aforesaid; — s.m. That which is described; a noun substantive, or (simply) a substantive (i.q. ism-ě-mauṣūf, or ism-mauṣūf)
more: adj. وصف described. 1. Described; named; celebrated. 2. Before-mentioned; above-mentioned. mausūf-ilaih . The afore-mentioned. ism mausūf , n. m. A noun substantive qualified by an adjective.
(part. pass. of وصف) adj. 1. Described, celebrated, praised; endowed. 2. Before-mentioned. 3. (subst.) A noun substantive qualified by an adjective.
subs: The afore-mentioned, the person before alluded to
v.t. To endow (with, -meṅ); to distinguish (by, or from among, -meṅ), &c.
(-se), To be distinguished, or characterized, &c. (by); to be endowed (with); to be praised.

ety: for A. موصوفة mauṣūfat, fem. of mauṣūf
def: part. = mauṣūf, q.v.; — s.m. Anything described, or particularized, or praised, &c.

part. adj. Joined; conjoined; coupled; connected; related; — reached (to), arrived (at); — attained; collected; received; — s.m. A collection; — (in Gram.) a conjunctive noun; the antecedent of a relative pronoun, (loosely) a relative pronoun (i.q. ism-ě-mauṣūl, or ism-mauṣūl).
more: n. m. ( Gram .) The antecedent to the relative.
part. pass. (of وصل) Joined, conjoined, connected; (in grammar) The relative pronoun, or the antecedent with respect to the relative.

A collection; — (in Gram.) a conjunctive noun; the antecedent of a relative pronoun, (loosely) a relative pronoun (i.q. ism-ě-mauṣūl, or ism-mauṣūl).
part. adj. Marked; signed; impressed; branded; — characterized; noted; stigmatized; — named, called, entitled

def: part. = mausūm, q.v. part. adj. Marked; signed; impressed; branded; — characterized; noted; stigmatized; — named, called, entitled
subs: (-ke nām-se), To call (by the name of), to name.

def: s.f. Death; — mortality
more: n. f. S. mritu . Death; mortality. Samajhne-vāle kī maut hai . Prov. It is death to those who understand it. maut ānā , v. n. To come (one's death); to die, 1. Kām karte maut ātī hai . He flies from work as from death. 2. Inko to patthar māre maut nahīṅ . Prov. Though he were stoned he would not die. maut bilā-qatl , n. f. A violent death. maut chāhnā , v. n. To wish for or seek death. maut kā pasīnā , n. m. lit . death sweats. Cold, clammy perspiration. Hichkī ātī hai, sard sīnah hai, Māthe par maut kā pasīnah hai . Shauq. The hiccup's on, cold feels his breast, Death sweats upon his forehead rest. maut kī sūrat banānā , v. n. To feign death. apnī maut marnā , v. n. To die a natural death. ba-hālat-i-maraz-ul-maut . G. G. On his death bed.
subs: To wish for, or to seek, death
To put on the appearance of death, to feign death
(-ko), Death to be at the heels (of); — to rush into the jaws of death, to court death
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موت (mawt), from Arabic مَوْت (mawt).

def: s.m. = موتي mautā, q.v. s.f. Death; — funeral obsequies; — death-like stillness or pallor
subs: (-ke), To go to, or to attend, the funeral (of).

def: s.f. Death; — funeral obsequies; — death-like stillness or pallor
more: n. f. See مردني 1, 2. mautā h. , v. n. To occur (a death).

Out on the man who to a fat wife's bound, Who fills the bed while he lies on the ground. 2. Jo supne māṅ dekhe jhoṅṭā, Sukh pāve, tan hove moṭā . Rus. If a buffalo calf in your dream you see Your lot will be ease and fat you will be. 2. Dense; thick. Bakkhar moṭā hai . 3. Thick; coarse; inferior. Moṭā kapṛā, nāj, yā kām . 4. Gross; great. Moṭī galtī . 5. Large; great. Moṭā kharch . 6. A capital (letter). Moṭe harf . 7. Palpable; plain. Moṭī bāt . 8. Material; important. 9. Rich; wealthy. moṭā-pan, muṭāpā , n. m. muṭāī , n. f. 1. Corpulency. 2. Thickness. 2. Coarseness. 4. Grossness. moṭā-tāzā , adj. Plump and fat; well-conditioned. moṭā dikhāī denā , v. n. To be dim-sighted. moṭā qalam . 1. A thic…

Like me (i.q. mujh-sā, or mere samān)
Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā; see kar)

To me; me (i.q. mujhe); — mo-hi, or mo-hī, Me indeed; to me truly, &c. (i.q. mujh-hī).

more: (noun of place of وطن) s. m. Birthplace, dwelling, home.

more: n. m. A parcel or parcels of land.
(noun of place from وضع Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. 1. A place, village, district. 2. Occurrence, conjuncture.

The part of a village superadded to the original

def: part. adj. Placed, situated; fixed, established; assigned; — taken for granted, assumed; — conventional (as a phrase, &c.); — s.m. Site, position, situation; — subject (of a speech, &c.); object (of a science, &c., e.g. mauẓūʻ-ě-ʻilm); (in Gram.) a word; — a postulate.
more: n. m. 1. ( Gram .) A word. 2. A postulate.
(part. pass. of وضع) s. m. A subject, object. adj. Established, placed, situated.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موضوع (mawzū'), from Arabic مَوْضُوع (mawḍūʕ).

ety: for A. موضوعة mauẓūʻat, fem. of mauẓūʻ
def: part. = mauẓūʻ, q.v. part. adj. Placed, situated; fixed, established; assigned; — taken for granted, assumed; — conventional (as a phrase, &c.); — s.m. Site, position, situation; — subject (of a speech, &c.); object (of a science, &c., e.g. mauẓūʻ-ě-ʻilm); (in Gram.) a word; — a postulate.
adj. 1. Well-balanced ( ṭhīk , 11). 2. Rythmical. mauzūṅ k. , v. a. To correct a verse.

ety: pass. part. of وزن 'to weigh exactly; to adjust,' &c.
def: part. adj. Weighed; balanced, well-adjusted; symmetrical; well-measured (verse), consisting of an exact number of feet; sythmical; — equable, equal; — modulated (sound), harmonious; — good, sweet, excellent, agreeable
more: (part. pass. of وزن Heb. ) adj. Weighed, well adjusted, symmetrical, well measured or of just quantity (verse).
subs: v.t. To adjust or correct the measure (of verses)
adj. Not rythmical; not well-measured (verse); ill-adjusted; — ill-adapted, or ill-chosen (words); disagreeable (speech).

def: s.f. The being weighed; the being well-balanced or adjusted; the being well-measured, or of proper quantity (verse); rhythm.

ety: prob. Prk. माउआ; S. मातृका; cf. Pers. māya
def: s.f. 'Source, origin'; — substance; — starch; — yolk (of an egg); — ferment, leaven: — milk inspissated by boiling; curd.
more: n. m. S. मण्ड 1. Substance. Māvā jamā-kar murgī anḍe detī hai . 2. Starch. 3. Leaven. 4. The yolk of an egg. 5. Milk inspissated by boiling; curd ( kundā , 1). māvā nikālnā , Slang, v. a. To beat to a jelly. Mere qābū meṅ āyā to mārte mārte māvā nikāl dūṅgā .
s. f. 1. Substance. 2. Starch. 3. The yolk of an egg. 4. Leaven. 5. Milk inspissated by boiling.
(from اوي) s. m. f. Dwelling, abode, habitation.

def: s. See ماوا Abode, &c.
(n. of place fr. أوي 'to repair to for lodging or refuge,' &c.), s.m. & f. Dwelling, abode, habitation, mansion; covert; place of resort; refuge, asylum.
s.m. = ماوا māwā, q.v. s.f. 'Source, origin'; — substance; — starch; — yolk (of an egg); — ferment, leaven: — milk inspissated by boiling; curd.
s.m. pl. (of mādda, q.v.), Matters, points, articles; — females; — humours (of the body); — matter, pus.
more: n. m. 1. Humours of the body. 2. Pus; matter. mavād-i-fāsid , Bad humours.
s. plur. (of مادّه) 1. Articles, matters, arguments, points. 2. Females.

ety: for A. مودة, v.n. fr. ودّ 'to love,' &c.
def: s.f. Love, affection; friendship.
more: (from ودّ Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Friendship, love.

ety: prob. pl. of موجب mūjab, pass. part. of اَوْجب 'to render necessary,' &c., iv of وجب 'to be necessary,' &c.
def: s.m. Salaries, pensions, rewards, allowances; hires, wages; — rights, dues.
more: (prop. ماوجب) s. m. Salaries, pensions, wages.

def: (for A. موالٍ mawāliṉ, pl. of مولَي maulā), s.m. pl. Lords, masters; princes; judges; friends; — slaves, servants.
more: s. plur. (of موليٰ) Lords, masters, friends, slaves. ¶ muwālī, part. act. (III. of ولي) Aider, assistant.

def: s.m. The three kingdoms of nature (the animal, vegetable, and mineral).
more: s. plur. (of مولود or مولد mūlid ) Sons. مواليد ثلاثه mawālīd-i-s̤alās̤a, s. m. The three kingdoms of nature (viz. animal, vegetable, and mineral).

def: s.m. pl. (of مانع māněʻ, q.v.), Impediments, obstacles, hindrances.
more: s. plur. (of مانع māniﻌ, q. v.) Impediments, obstacles.

def: s.m. pl. of and = mawāněʻ, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = mumānī, q.v. s.f. Mother's brother's wife, aunt (i.q. māmī, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Husband's, or wife's, maternal aunt (i.q. mamiyā-sās).

def: s.m. Protection, refuge, asylum, retreat; — a collection of trees, a shady grove or wood.
more: s. m. Protection, refuge, asylum, retreat.

def: s.f. contrac. of amāwas, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of ماشية māshiyat), Quadrupeds, cattle (esp. camels, cows, sheep, and the like; cf. maweshī).
more: s. plur. (of ماشية from مشي) Quadrupeds, cattle.

def: s.f. A desert; waste land.
s.m. pl. (of mauẓaʻ, q.v.), Places; villages.
s.m. pl. of and = mawāẓěʻ, q.v.
s.f. A herd or drove of oxen; — cattle; — goats; sheep
more: n. f. S. महिष buffalo. Goats and sheep; cattle. maveshī charānā , v. n. To depasture cattle. maveshī-khānā , n. m. 1. A cattle pen. 2. A pound.
(corrupt. of مواشي Sans. महिष ) s. f. A herd or drove of oxen.
subs: To graze cattle; to tend cattle while grazing

def: s.m. A dried grape, a raisin.
adj. Waving; billowy, surgy; boisterous, stormy, raging (sea).
more: adj. (superl. of موج Heb. ) Waving, boisterous, billowy; raging (sea).

ety: Pehl. māi; Zend madhu; S. मधु
def: s.f. Wine; spirituous liquor
subs: adj. & s.m. Addicted to wine; — a lover of wine; a drunkard
s.f. The being addicted to wine, love of wine; a symposium
s.m. A wine-cellar; a tavern; a spirit-shop
or mai-ḵẖẉār, s.m. A wine-bibber, a sot
or mai-ḵẖẉārī, or mai-ḵẖẉāragī, s.f. Wine-drinking; carousal; drunkenness

def: s.m. Name of an Asur (described as the artificer or architect of the Daityas); — a horse; camel; mule

s.f. Extraordinary or supernatural power, magical or wonderful power; — illusion; trick, artifice, deceit, deception, fraud; delusion; jugglery; wickedness, villainy; — an illusory image or apparition, phantasm, phantom; anything unreal; a mirage; anything which deceives the sight; a show; — a deception depending on the power of the deity whereby mankind believe in the existence of external objects which are in fact purely ideal; the external world, natural phenomena, nature (considered as a mere illusion without reality, and personified in mythology as a female, the wife of Brahmā, and the immediate operative cause of the creation); — philosophic illusion, idealism; — the wife of a juggler; — a woman; — prosperity, opulence, riches; life; light; — compassion, pity, sympathy, mercy, kindness, affection, feeling
more: n. f. S. माया. 1. The delusive appearance of external objects, which are in fact nothing but pictures of the mind. Parmeshuar kī māyā hai . 2. Mirage; delusion; idle fancy. 3. The works of nature; Nature; natural phenomena. Janlo na jāī tor māyā, mor Prabhu jī! ihavāṅ andhār, tau haṅvāṅ ujiyār ! Bhoj. Song. Thy illusions, O Lord! pass all comprehension! here it's darkness, there light! 4. Light; life; show. Chār paise kī māyā hai māyā-rūpī , adj. Illusory; false. Is māyā-rūpī sansār meṅ kyoṅ bhiram rahā hai ?

s. f. 1. Kindness, pity, sympathy, compassion, mercy, feeling, affection. 2. A deception depending on the power of the Deity, whereby mankind believe in the existence of external objects, which are in fact nothing but idea; illusion, idealism. 3. Prosperity, riches, opulence. مايا پاتر māyā-pātra, adj. Rich, opulent, wealthy.
s. m. for مايه māya, q. v.
subs: s.f. A place of deception; a locality where wickedness abounds
s.m. 'A vessel of wealth'; a wealthy person; — adj. Opulent, wealthy, rich
s.m. 'Lord of illusion'; a master in illusions; the husband of Illusion personified; an epithet of the deity or of Rāma
adj. & s.m.f. Skilled in illusion, or in magical arts; expert in deceiving; delusive, deceptive, fraudulent
(˚yā+at˚), adj. Beyond the influence of illusion (said of the saints and the deity); — beyond the influence of compassion, without compassion, merciless, unfeeling (syn. māyā-rahit)
or māyā-rūpī, adj. Illusory, unreal, false, deceptive
adj. (f. -ā), Without compassion, pitiless, merciless, unfeeling; unloving
adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of illusion; formed of or creating illusion; illusive, illusory, unreal, deceptive; enchanted; magical; wonderful

māyā-vant, or māyā-vān, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Possessing powers of illusion; having magical powers; skilled in magical arts; employing deceit, or tricks; deceptive; magical; illusory, unreal; — a deceiver, &c.; — an epithet of Kans; — māyā-vatī, s.f. A female deceiver; — an epithet of the wife of Kām
s.f. The science of illusion; magic.
wety: From Sanskrit माया (māyā́).

def: s.m. = مايه māya, q.v. s.f. Extraordinary or supernatural power, magical or wonderful power; — illusion; trick, artifice, deceit, deception, fraud; delusion; jugglery; wickedness, villainy; — an illusory image or apparition, phantasm, phantom; anything unreal; a mirage; anything which deceives the sight; a show; — a deception depending on the power of the deity whereby mankind believe in the existence of external objects which are in fact purely ideal; the external world, natural phenomena, nature (considered as a mere illusion without reality, and personified in mythology as a female, the wife of Brahmā, and the immediate operative cause of the creation); — philosophic illusion, idealism; — the wife of a juggler; — a woman; — prosperity, opulence, riches; life; light; — compassion, pity, sympathy, mercy, kindness, affection, feeling
subs: s.f. A place of deception; a locality where wickedness abounds
s.m. 'A vessel of wealth'; a wealthy person; — adj. Opulent, wealthy, rich
s.m. 'Lord of illusion'; a master in illusions; the husband of Illusion personified; an epithet of the deity or of Rāma
adj. & s.m.f. Skilled in illusion, or in magical arts; expert in deceiving; delusive, deceptive, fraudulent
(˚yā+at˚), adj. Beyond the influence of illusion (said of the saints and the deity); — beyond the influence of compassion, without compassion, merciless, unfeeling (syn. māyā-rahit)
or māyā-rūpī, adj. Illusory, unreal, false, deceptive
adj. (f. -ā), Without compassion, pitiless, merciless, unfeeling; unloving
adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of illusion; formed of or creating illusion; illusive, illusory, unreal, deceptive; enchanted; magical; wonderful

māyā-vant, or māyā-vān, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Possessing powers of illusion; having magical powers; skilled in magical arts; employing deceit, or tricks; deceptive; magical; illusory, unreal; — a deceiver, &c.; — an epithet of Kans; — māyā-vatī, s.f. A female deceiver; — an epithet of the wife of Kām
s.f. The science of illusion; magic.
wety: From Sanskrit माया (māyā́).

def: s.m. Source, root, origin, principle, essence, substance (cf. māwā); wealth, money, cash, means; capital (in trade), stock, principal; fund; — measure; quantity; amount; — ferment, leaven
more: n. f. Wealth ( pūṅjī ). 1. Kāyā baṛī, ki māyā ? Prov. Is your body of more value or your wealth? (Said to a miser). 2. Tulsī Dās garīb kī kohū na pūchhe bāt, Māyā se māyā mile kar-ke lambe hāth . Tulsi Dās. Poor Tulsi Das no one doth care about, Rich men to rich men sole their hands stretch out. 3. Māyā huī to kyā huā, hirdā huā kaṭhor, Nau neze pānī chaṛhā tau bhī na bhīgī kor ? What boots his wealth if hard-hearted he yet remain? Nine fathoms deep are naught if dryness still pertain. māyā joṛnā , v. n. To hoard money.
s. m. 1. Stock, capital, fund, wealth, means. 2. Quantity. 3. Leaven, ferment. 4. Essence, origin.
subs: To amass wealth; to hoard money
adj. Thick, solid, strong, substantial; rich, wealthy; — fermented.

def: s.f. contrac. of māyā, 'pity,' &c., q.v.
more: s. f. Kindness, pity, sympathy, compassion, mercy, feeling, affection. See مايا māyā.

def: adj. ميل inclined towards. 1. Inclined or leaning towards; bent; apt. 2. Partial; biassed. 3. ( In Comp .) English, as zardī-māyal , yellowish. māyal k. , v. a. To incline to ( rujū k . 1 and 2).
more: (part. act. of ميل) adj. Inclined, bent, inclined towards, fond, desirous, taking delight in, affectionate, having a propensity or inclination or partiality; addicted, apt. مايل کرنا maʼịl karnā, v. a. To incline, bend, bias, &c. مايل هونا māʼịl honā, v. n. To incline.

def: s.m. Inclining (to or towards, -kī t̤araf), being propense; — inclination, &c. (i.q. mail, q.v.); — loving.

ety: Prk. मयंकु or मयंको; S. मृग+अङ्कः
def: s.m. 'The deer-spotted,' the moon.
adv. (dialec.) = majhāre (obl. of majhārā), and majhile (obl. of majhilā), qq.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessing illusion, illusory, unreal; deceptive; possessing magical powers; magical; using tricks, employing deceit, deceitful; — a deceiver; a magician, conjurer, juggler; — a cat.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessing illusion, illusory, unreal; deceptive; possessing magical powers; magical; using tricks, employing deceit, deceitful; — a deceiver; a magician, conjurer, juggler; — a cat.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessing powers of illusion; having supernatural powers; illusory, unreal, deceptive, magical; — a conjurer, juggler, magician; a deceiver, cheat; — an epithet of Brahmā, of Śiva, of Agni, and of Kāma.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Illusory; creating illusion; practising deceit, deceitful, deceptive, trickish; — fascinating; — compassionate; affectionate; benevolent; — a magician, conjurer, juggler.
part. adj. Faulty, defective, blemished; — found fault with; accused (of); stigmatized; — blamable, reprehensible; vicious; opprobrious, infamous.
adj. عيب 1. Bad; evil; ill. 2. Opprobrious; improper, reprehensible.
more: (part. pass. of عيب) adj. Wicked, vitious, defective, opprobrious, infamous, blamable, reprehensible.

def: s.f. Faultiness; wickedness, vice, &c.
s.m. A dialec. form of the next, q.v.
s.m. The pin or gnomon of a sun-dial (i.q. meḵẖ, and kīl); — a ray of light; light; brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; beauty; — flame.
s.m. (f. -ī), A peacock (i.q. mor, q.v.); — the flower Cock's-comb, Celosia cristata; — the plant Achyranthes aspera
subs: vulg. mayūrārī (˚ra+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of the peacock,' the chameleon; a lizard
or ćūṛā, or ćūrā, s.f. The cock's-comb, Celosia cristata.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मयूर (mayūra). Doublet of مور (mor).

def: s.m. corr. of ميور mayūr, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A peacock (i.q. mor, q.v.); — the flower Cock's-comb, Celosia cristata; — the plant Achyranthes aspera
subs: vulg. mayūrārī (˚ra+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of the peacock,' the chameleon; a lizard
or ćūṛā, or ćūrā, s.f. The cock's-comb, Celosia cristata.

def: part. (post classical), fr. يَئِسَ 'to despair,' &c.), part. adj. Disappointed, despondent, hopeless, desperate
more: adj. Disappointed; hopeless; despondent. māyūs k. , v. a. To disappoint; mar one's hopes. māyūs h. , v. n. To be disappointed, or hopeless.
(part. pass. of ياس Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Desperate, hopeless.
subs: v.t. To disappoint, to make despondent or despairing; to mar the hopes of
v.n. To despair, lose hope, to be disappointed (of, -se).
wety: Borrowed from Persian مایوس, from Arabic مأيوس.

def: s.f. Hopelessness, despair; desperation.

ety: prob. Zend mazaṅh; S. महस्; but cf. A. mazzat, inf. n. of un. of مزّ 'to suck,' &c.
def: s.m. Taste savour, smack, relish; delight, pleasure, enjoyment; anything agreeable to the palate or to the mind, &c.; a delicacy, a tidbit; a bon-mot; jest, joke, fun, sport, amusement
more: n. m. 1. See تماشه 2 & 3, and zāëqā . 2. Taste ( chaskā ). 3. Pleasure; enjoyment ( bahār , 4). 1. Chār paise bigāṛte haiṅ jab yeh maze dekhte haiṅ . When I four pice can throw away then I enjoy this pleasure may. 2. Mazā barsāt kā chāho to baiṭho merī āṅkhoṅ meṅ, Safedī hai, siyāhī hai, shafaq hai, abr o bārāṅ hai . Would you the rains enjoy, come in my eyes and dwell, Rife here are fair, black, rosy dawn, and showers as well. mazā uṛānā, lenā, yā lūṭnā , v. n. To enjoy; revel. Kyoṅ jī! tum to maze uṛāo, aur ham baiṭhe jaleṅ! mazā chakhānā, yā dikhānā , v. a. See tamāsha dikhānā. Kar-ke yār inko chakhā dūṅ ranḍī-bāzī kā mazā , Wom. Rāhat. Lekin, aë Khairan! nahīṅ ātā baṛhānā shar mujhe . A lover I might take, and spoil my husband's rakish joys, But 'tis not in me, dear, to fan the flame which peace destroys. maze k. , v. a. To enjoy oneself ( tamāshā k. , 1). mazā khanḍat ho jānā , v. n…

s. m. Taste, flavour, relish, delight, pleasure, enjoyment, any thing agreeable to the palate or mind, &c. a delicacy, a tidbit or bon mot. مزه دار maza-dār (or dakh. مزي دار maze-dār ) adj. Delicious, tasteful, relishing, palatable.
subs: v.n. To give free scope to pleasure or enjoyment, to enjoy oneself, to revel (in)
or maza dikhānā (-ko), To give (one) a taste (of, -kā); to show (one) some sport or fun, to afford (one) enjoyment; (ironic.) to spoil the pleasure, or enjoyment, or sport (of)
vulg. maze-dār, adj. Tasty, savoury, tasteful, palatable, delicious
vulg. maze-dārī, s.f. Tastiness, savouriness, deliciousness; — taste, flavour; — pleasure, enjoyment
To enjoy oneself; to enjoy or amuse oneself (at the expense of), to make sport or fun (of), to laugh (at)
adj. (f. -kī) = maza-dār, q.v.; — maze-kī bāt, A thing that affords pleasure, or amusement; anything strange or funny; a jest, joke, pleasantry
or maza lūṭnā, To take pleasure, to enjoy ooneself, to revel (syn. maza uṛānā)
adj. Tasteless, insipid, flavourless, vapid; — out of humour, displeased, offended.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مزه (maza).

def: What is passed is passed; we will say no more about it; let bygones be bygones.
s.m. corr. of مزه maza, q.v.
more: s. m. Taste, flavour. See مزه.

r: maẓā, maẓā mā maẓā, jo huʼā so huʼā
def: (3rd pers. sing. perf. tense), It is past
subs: Let what is passed be passed; let bygones be bygones; let us speak no more of it (i.q. jo huʼā so huʼā).

def: s.m. pl. of مزبله mazbala, q.v.
more: s. plur. of مزبله mazbala, q. v.

def: s.m. pl. (of maẕhab, q.v.), Ways by which one goes; — religions, creeds, sects; — institutes.
s.m. pl. (of maz̤har, q.v.), Manifestations, views; objects; spectacles.

ety: inf. n., and also n. of place, fr. ذاق (for ذوق
def: s.m. Tasting
more: n. m. ذرق tasted. 1. ( Ped .) Taste ( chaskā , 1). 2. Wit; humour; pleasantry ( ṭhaṭṭā , 2, 3). mazāq se, mazāqan , adv. Facetiously; humorously. mazāq kā ādmī , n. m. A humourist; a wit. mazāq'yā , adj. Witty; humorous.
(noun of place from ذوق Aram. ) s. m. 1. Taste, tasting. 2. The palate.
subs: relish; — taste; nice perception; wit, humour, pleasantry; — the place or seat of taste, the palate
adv. With taste, with nice perception; wittily, facetiously, humorously
A man of taste; a humorist, wit.
ex: میں اسکا برا مذاق برداشت نہیں کروںگا۔
I will not tolerate his bad joke.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَذَاق (mazāq), from Arabic مَذَاق (maḏāq).

def: adv. = maẕāq-se, q.v.s.v. maẕāq.

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. muzāḥ, q.v.
def: s.f. Jest, joke, fun, pleasantry
more: (corrupt. of مزاح mizāḥ, from مزح) s. m. A jest, a joke, pleasantry. مزاخين کرنا mazạ̄ḵẖeṅ karnā, To make jokes, to jest.

ety: for A. مذاکرة maẕākirat, pl. of maẕkarat; see maẕkara
def: s.m. Things worthy to be remembered (especially sacred).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُذَاکَرَه (muzākara).

ety: maẕāq, q.v. + iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of fine taste, of nice perception; witty, humorous; — a man of fine taste, &c. ( = maz̤āq-kā ādmī).

ety: for A. مذلة, inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base, or abject,' &c.
def: s.f. Baseness, vileness, abjectness, contemptibleness, contempt, ignominiousness; ignominy, &c.
more: (from ذلّ Heb. ؟) s. f. Baseness, contempt, abjectness.

def: s.m. pl. (of maẓmūn, q.v.), Contents (of a letter, &c.); things implied or meant; significations.
s.f.(?) pl. (of mazmūr), Psalms; can ticles.
s.f. pl. (of mizmār), Flutes; psalteries; an organ.

ety: for A. مذمة, inf. n. of ذمّ 'to blame,' &c.
def: s.f. Blaming; blame, censure; scorn, contempt; satire; abuse; evil-speaking; — anything deserving censure, a blamable or discommendable qnality or action, a fault
more: n. f. ذم blamed. 1. Evil-speaking. 2. Satire; lampoon.
(inf. from ذمّ) s. f. Blame, satire, abuse, scorn, contempt.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مذمت (mazammat), from Arabic مَذَمَّة (maḏamma).

def: (n. of place fr. زار (for زور) 'to visit'), s.m. & f. A place of visitation; — a shrine; a sepulchre, tomb, grave.
more: (noun of place from زور Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. f. 1. A place of visitation; (hence) 2. Sepulchre, tomb, grave, shrine.

def: s.m. pl. (of mazraʻa, q.v.), Places of seed-produce; sown fields, lands in tillage.
more: n. m. زرع he sowed. A husbandman; a cultivator.
(part. act. III. of زرع) s. m. A husbandman. ¶ mazāriﻌ, s. plur. of مزرع mazraﻌ, q. v.

ety: for A. مضرة, v.n. fr. ضرّ 'to injure,' &c.
def: s.f. A cause or means of injury, or harm, or mischief, &c.; harm, injury, hurt, detriment, damage, inconvenience
more: n. f. ضرر Injury (ضرر). mazarrat pahuṅchānā , v. a. To injure; hurt; damage. mazarrat-rasānī , n. f. Injuring another.
(inf. of ضرر Heb. ) s. f. Detriment, damage, injury.
subs: (-ko), To cause injury, &c. to reach (a person, &c.), to injure, hurt, damage
s.f. Causing injury to reach (another), injuring, harming, hurting.

ety: for A. مزبلة mazbalat, n. of place fr. زبل 'to dung,' &c.
def: s.m. A place where dung is thrown, or where the filth and sweepings (of a house) are thrown; a dunghill; a necessary, a privy.
more: (noun of place from زبل) s. m. A privy, a necessary, a dunghill, any place where filth is thrown.

ety: n. of place fr. ذبح 'to slaughter'
def: s.m. A slaughter-house; place where an animal is sacrificed; an altar.
more: (prop. maẕbaḥ, noun of place from ذبح) s. m. A place of sacrifice, an altar.

def: part. Slaughtered, sacrificed; slain, killed; — pure, lawful (food).
more: (part. pass. of ذبح Heb. Aram. ) adj. Killed, slain, sacrificed. (Plur. مذبوحات maẕbūḥāt ).

def: s.m. pl. (of maẕbūḥ), Animals slaughtered or sacrificed; victims.
s. State of being killed, &c.
s.f. The state of being slaughtered, &c.

ety: pass. part. of زبر 'to write' (a book, &c.
def: part. Written; — above-mentioned, aforesaid (cf. maẕkūr).
more: (part. pass. of زبر Heb. ) adj. Written, above-mentioned, forementioned.

ety: pass. part. of ضبط 'to take, or hold firmly; to restrain,' &c.
def: part. adj. & adv. Held firmly; possessed, occupied; — restrained, controlled; well regulated; moderated; disciplined; — firm, fixed, fast, tight; immovable; stable, steady; strong, stout; — resolute; cogent, valid; — firmly, tightly, &c.
more: adj. ضبط See پکا 5, 10, 18, 20, 21, sakht , 1, & sangīṅ , 3. 1. Strong; stout. 2. Fixed; immoveable; stable; steady. 3. Resolute. 4. Cogent; valid. mazbūt rahnā , v. n. To be fixed or firm. mazbūt k. , v. a. 1. To make strong or firm. 2 To fix; make fast.
(part. pass. of ضبط) adj. 1. Strong, firm, fixed, fast. 2. Possessed, occupied, restrained, moderated.
subs: v.t. To make firm, or strong, &c.; to make fast, to fix.
wety: From Arabic مَضْبُوط (maḍbūṭ).

def: s.f. Strength; force; firmness; solidity; durability; validity; cogency
more: n. f. 1. Strength; firmness; force. 2. Solidity. 3. Validity; cogency.

ety: n. of place fr. ذهب 'to go, pass along,' &c.
def: s.m. Way, course, mode, or manner, of acting or conduct or the like; — canon, law, rule, institution; — a belief, creed, persuasion, doctrine; an opinion, a tenet; a body of tenets or articles of belief, a religion; a sect
more: (noun of place from ذهب) s. m. Religion, sect, way, mode. (Plur. مذاهب Dual. inflect. مذهبين maẕʼhabain ).
subs: (-kā), To spread or propagate the religion (of)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَذْهَب (mazhab), from Arabic مَذْهَب (maḏhab).

def: n. m. Religious belief; creed; faith. mazhab badalnā , v. n. To change one's religion. mazhab phailānā , v. a. To propagate, or spread a religion. mazhab qāyam rakhnā , v. a. To maintain a religion. mazhab meṅ milānā , v. a. To convert to a religion. aqīdah-i-mazhab . G. G. Religious belief. muāfī-i-mazhab . G. G. A religious grant.

adj. Religious. mazhabī ; Illit. mazbī , n. m. 1. One who is a Sikh by religion. 2. A Sikh of the sweeper caste. mazhabī jhagṛā , A religious dispute. mazhabī laṛāī . A religious war; a crusade. mazhabī muāmlā . A religious matter or dispute. dars-i-mazhabī . Religious teaching; preaching. masala-i-mazhabī . Religious tenets.
adj. Of or relating to a religion, or sect, &c.; religious; — s.m. One who is a Sikh by religion; — a Sikh of the sweeper caste
subs: s.f. A religious war; a crusade
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مذهبی (mazhabī). Equivalent to مذہب (mazhab, “religion, faith”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: for A. مضحکة maẓḥakat, v.n. fr. اضحك 'to cause to laugh'
def: s.m. A cause of laughter, a thing at which one laughs, or which one ridicules; — pleasantry, humour, fun, drollery; a pun, a bon-mot, &c.
more: n. m. ضحک Pleasantry; banter; drollery.
(from ضحک) s. m. Humour, fun, drollery, a pun, bon mot, laughingstock.

ety: n. of place fr. ظہر 'to become apparent, or manifest,' &c.
def: s.m. A place where any spectacle is represented; theatre; ampitheatre; stage; the scene (of a deed, &c.); the object (of divine vengeance, &c.); manifestation; object; view.
more: (noun of place of ظہر Heb. ) s. m. A place where any spectacle is represented, a theatre, stage; object, view. ¶ muz̤ʼhir (part. act. IV.) adj. Exhibiting; (subst.) An exhibiter, displayer, proposer.

ety: act. part. of مضي 'to pass by'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Passing by, past; — past time, the past; — the preterite or past tense (also feʻl-ě-māẓī); — māẓī istimrārī, The past imperfect; the past progressive or continuous, the past habitual
more: adj. مضو proceeding. ( Gram .) Past time. māzī-i-ihtimālī yā shakkī , ( Gram .) The past subjunctive.

(part. act. of مضو) 1. adj. Past (time). 2. (or فعل ماضي) s. m. Preterite tense. ماضي استمراري māẓī istimrārī, Preterimperfect. ماضي بعيد māẓī baʼīﻌd, Preterpluperfect. ماضي قريب māẓī ḳarīb, Preterperfect. ماضي معطوف عليه māẓī maﻌt̤ūf aﻌlaihi, Past conjunctive participle.
subs: or māẓī shakkī, or māẓī ěḥtimālī, The past subjunctive, the past dubious
The absolute or indefinite past, the preterite positive
or māẓī maʻt̤ūfa, The past conjunctive participle
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ماضی (māzī), from Arabic مَاضِي (māḍī). Cognate with Gujarati માજી (mājī), Kannada ಮಾಜಿ (māji), Marathi माजी (mājī), Telugu మాజీ- (mājī-).

def: n. f. ( Gram ) The pluperfect tense.
n. f. ( Gram .) The perfect tense.
( Gram .) The past conjunctive participle.
n. f. ( Gram .) The preterite or past tense.
s.m. Increase, augmentation; — advantage
more: n. m. زيد increased. Increase; augmentation.
(from زيد Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. Increase, augmentation, advantage. مزيد کرنا mazīd karnā, To take away the dinner, tablecloth, &c.
subs: v.t. To increase; — to remove or take away (the dinner, or table-cloth, &c.; syn. baṛhānā)
(pass. part. of the same verb), part. Increased, augmented; — mazīd fī-hi, Idem.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَزِید (mazīd), from Arabic مَزِيد (mazīd). Compare Punjabi مَزِید (mazīd) / ਮਜ਼ੀਦ (mazīd).

def: n. f. ( Gram .) The past progressive.

ety: for A. مظنة maz̤innat, n. of place fr. ظنّ 'to think, opine, suppose,' &c.
def: s.m. Cause of thinking or supposing the existence (of); indication or evidence of the existence (of a thing, or a quality in one); — opinion, thought; suspicion, conjecture.
more: (from ظنّ) s. m. Suspicion, conjecture, opinion.

ety: v.n. fr. مزر 'to be angry against,' &c.
def: adj. Strong, bold, stout-hearted, fearless, intrepid.

ety: for A. معذرة, inf. n. and v.n. of عذر 'to excuse'
def: s.f. Excuse, apology; plea (i.q. ʻuẕr, q.v.). s.m. Excuse, apology, plea; pretext; objection; — the attainment or accomplishment of one's wants or wishes, success, fortunate issue
subs: v.n. To leave no ground for excuse or objection; to leave no objection unanswered
v.n. To bring forward, or to offer, an excuse or an apology; to plead; to raise an objection, to object (to)
adj. & s.m. Apologizing; apologetic; — apologist; one who begs pardon (of another)
s.f. An excuse, apology; excusing oneself for not attending a funeral; — condolence (syn. pursā); — ʻuẕr-ḵẖẉāhī karnā (-kī), To make excuse or apology; to excuse oneself (from); to apologize
adj. & s.m. Having a plea; producing an excuse; objecting; — an objector; a claimant
s.f. The having a plea, producing an excuse; a statement of objections; a cross-demand, a caveat
v.t. To make an excuse; to apologize; to take exception (to, -kī), to contest
(-kī), To bring forward an excuse, to plead excuses; to apologize; to raise objections (to)
or qābil-ě-ʻuẕr, adj. Excusable, pardonable; objectionable
s.f. Excuses, apologies; — ʻuẕr-maʻẕarat karnā, v.n. To make excuses; to make an apology; to pray (against, -kī), to deprecate
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْذِرَت (ma'zirat), borrowed from Arabic مَعْذِرَة (maʕḏira), from عَذَر (ʕaḏar). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi معذرت (m'ẕrt /⁠ma'ẕirat⁠/).

def: G. G. Heretofore.

ety: for A. مزية, v.n. fr. مزي to be proud'
def: s.f. Excess, superabundance; — excellence.

ety: for A. مذکرة maẕkarat, v.n. fr. ذکر 'to remember; to mention'
def: s.m. Anything worthy to be remembered (esp. sacred); — mentioning, relating; mention, relation.

ety: pass. part. of ذکر 'to remember; to mention'
def: part. Remembered; recorded; mentioned, expressed, related, said; fore-mentioned, aforesaid, afore-named; — s.m. Mention, relation, account, discourse, statement; the contents or substance of a written statement
more: adj. ذکر remembered. Mentioned; said. mazkūr ; pl. mazkūrāt , n. m. A statement; the contents or substance of a written statement. mazkūrah bālā, mazkūr-us-sadr , adv. Aforesaid; above-mentioned.

(part. pass. of ذکر) 1. adj. Forementioned, aforesaid, said, mentioned, remembered. 2. s. m. Mention, discourse. مذکور کرنا maẕkūr karnā, To mention. Plur. مذکورات maẕkūrāt, Discourses, relations, matters mentioned.
subs: adj. Above-mentioned, aforesaid: maẕkūr karnā(-kā), To make mention (of), to mention, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَذْکُور (mazkūr), from Arabic مَذْكُور (maḏkūr). First attested in c. 1635 Middle Hindi مذكور (mazkūr).

ety: for A. مذکورة maẕkūrat, fem. of maz̤kūr
def: adj. Remembered, &c. ( = maẕkūr, q.v.)
s.m. pl. (of maẕkūr), Relations, discourses, matters mentioned, statements.
adj. & s.m. Process-serving; — a process-server.

ety: for A. مظلمة maz̤limat, &c., inf. n. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully, or unjustly,' &c.
def: s.m. Wrongdoing, iniquity, oppression, injustice, tyranny, cruelty.
more: (from ظلم) s. m. Injustice, oppression, tyranny.

ety: pass. part. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully,' &c.; see maz̤lama, and z̤ulm
def: part. Wronged, treated or used wrongfully, or unjustly, or tyrannically, &c.; injured; oppressed; — meek, mild; gentle; modest; — s.m. One who is treated wrongfully, or unjustly, &c.; a victim of oppression
more: (part. pass. of ظلم) adj. 1. Injured, oppressed. 2. Mild, gentle, modest. مظلوم نوازي maz̤lūm-nawāz-ī, s. f. The cherishing of the oppressed.
subs: s.f. Cherishing or protecting the oppressed.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَظْلُوم (maẓlūm).

ety: maz̤lūm, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ; — and ˚īyat, A. abst. aff. formed fr. the rel. adj.
def: s.f. Oppression, tyranny; misery; the state of one oppressed; — meekness, mildness, subduedness, gentleness; modesty.
more: (from ظلم) s. f. Modesty, the state of being oppressed.

ety: maz̤lūm, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ; — and ˚īyat, A. abst. aff. formed fr. the rel. adj.
def: s.f. Oppression, tyranny; misery; the state of one oppressed; — meekness, mildness, subduedness, gentleness; modesty.
more: (from ظلم) s. f. Modesty, the state of being oppressed.

ety: for A. مزمزة mazmazat, inf. n. of the quadr. v مزمز 'to shake to and fro'
def: s.m. Shaking to and fro; putting in motion.

ety: for A. مضمضة maẓmaẓat, inf. n. of the quadr. v مضمض
def: s.m. Washing, or rinsing (the mouth, or a vessel).
more: s. m. Washing or rinsing out the mouth.

def: part. adj. Blamed, dispraised, discommended, found fault with, censured, reprehended; despised, scorned — blamable; contemptible, base, mean.
more: (part. pass. of ذمّ) adj. Scorned, contemptible, despised.

ety: pass. part. of ضمّ 'to draw, or bring together, to collect,' &c.
def: part. Added, joined; accumulated; compressed, drawn in; — marked or pronounced with the vowel ẓamma (a letter).
more: adj. ضم ( Gram .) A letter pronounced with the vowel point zamm or pesh (/?/).
(part. pass. of ضمّ Chald. ) adj. 1. Added, joined, accumulated. 2. (A letter) pronounced with the vowel point ẓamm or pesh (ُ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مضموم (mazmūm), from Arabic مَضْمُوم (maḍmūm).

ety: pass. part. of ضمن 'to contain, include,' &c.
def: s.m. That which is included or comprised (in a thing); the contents (of a writing or letter), what is implied (in a writing, &c.), import, sense, signification, meaning, tenour; — subject-matter, subject; — a composition, an article, an essay, an address
more: n. m. ضمن 1. The contents (of a writing). 2. Subject; matter. 3. A composition; an article; essay; address. 4. The sense; purport; tenor (معني). mazmūṅ-nigār . 1. An essayist. 2. An editor. mazmūṅ-nigārī , n. f. 1. Essay-writing. 2. Editorship.
(part. pass. of ضمن) s. m. Contents (of a letter, &c.), import, sense, signification, meaning, tenour.
subs: s.m. Writer of an article; an essayist; — an editor
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مضمون (mazmūn), from Arabic مَضْمُون (maḍmūn).

def: (fr. زمر; see mizmār), s.f.(?) A psalm, canticle.
s.m. contrac. of next, q.v.
more: (noun of place from زرع) s. m. A field sown or prepared for sowing, corn-field.

def: s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: for A. مزرعة mazraʻat, n. of place fr. زرع 'to sow,' &c.
def: s.m. A place of seed-produce; a corn-field; a field sown or prepared for sowing; — a farm; a hamlet.
more: n. m. 1. A hamlet. 2. A farm.

def: part. Sown; tilled, cultivated; under cultivation; — s.m. A sown field; — seed-produce that is watered by the rain.
more: (part. pass. of زرع Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Sown, cultivated.

ety: for A. مزروعة mazrūʻat, fem. of mazrūʻ
def: part. adj. Sown, &c. ( = mazrūʻ); — s.m. A sown field, or a field with a crop on it; cultivated land.
more: adj. Cultivated; tilled. mazrūah , n. m. Cultivated land; a field with a crop on it.

ety: pass. part. of ضرب 'to multiply' one number by another
def: part. Multiplied; — s.m.(?) (in Arith.) The multiplicand
s.m. Gall-nut; oak-apple.
more: s. m. The oakapple, or gall. See ماجوپهل mājū-phal.

ety: pass. part. of عزل 'to remove, depose, displace,' &c.
def: part. Removed, deposed, displaced, dismissed (from office); degraded; dethroned
more: adj. عزل Deposed; dismissed (from office). māzūl k. , v. a. To discharge; dismiss (from office). māzūl h. , v. n. To be dismissed ( bartaraf h .)
(part. pass. of عزل) adj. Deposed, displaced, dismissed, degraded, removed from office. معزول کرنا maﻌzūl karnā, To discharge, dismiss, remove (from office), disgrace.
subs: or kar-denā, v.t. To remove, discharge, or dismiss (-se, from office), to depose; to dethrone; to degrade, or disgrace

def: s.f. Removal (from office), dismissal; deposition; dethronement; — disgrace.
more: n. f. Dismissal ( bartarfī ). māzūlī lāzim ānā , v. n. To involve dismissal.
s. f. Dismission from office, &c., disgrace.

def: part. adj. Excused; — excusable; — exempted (from); — dispensed with; — helpless, powerless; — unserviceable; — disappointed
more: adj. 1. Excused; excusable. 2. Dispensed with. 3. Unserviceable. māzūr-ul-khidmat , adj. Pensioned; invalided. māzūr rakhnā , v. n. 1. To excuse; to hold excusable. 2. To dispense with. māzūr'ī , n. f. Helplessness; inability.
(part. pass. of عذر) adj. 1. Excused, excusable. 2. Disappointed.
subs: Excused or exempted from service; invalided, pensioned
v.t. To hold excused, or excusable; to excuse; — to exempt (from, -se)
wety: First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi معذور (maʻẕūr), from Classical Persian معذور (maʻẕūr), itself from Arabic مَعْذُور (maʕḏūr).

def: s.m. corr. of mazdūr, q.v.
more: (corrupt. of مزدور) s. m. A labourer.
wety: Corrupted form of مَزْدُور (mazdūr).

def: s.f. corr. of mazdūrī, q.v.
more: s. f. Pay of labour, wages.

def: s.f. Excusableness; exemption (from); — helplessness, inability.
To excuse.
s.m. A gut, an intestine.

ety: n. of instr. fr. عبر 'to cross'
def: s.m. A thing upon which, or by means of which, one crosses a river; a ferry-boat; — a pontoon; a bridge.
s.m. (dialec.) = manć, and maćān, qq.v. (cf. mej = mez).
adj. Dark blue, dark-coloured, black; — s.m. Dark blue colour, black; blackness; — smoke; a cloud; — an eye of a peacock's tail; — sulphuret of antimony.
s.f. Blackness, darkness.
s.m. corr. of ميدهہ medh, q.v. s.m. Offering, oblation, sacrifice.
more: s. m. 1. ( मेध ) Sacrifice. 2. ( मेदः ) Marrow. 3. Excessive corpulency.

def: s.f. A species of root resembling ginger (used as a drug, especially in cases of fever and consumption; it is brought from the Morung district, and is one of the eight principal medicaments)
s.m. Fat, adeps; marrow; lymph, serum; — corpulence, excessive fatness; morbid or unnatural corpulence
subs: s.f. A membrane in the abdomen containing the fat, the omentum
s.f. Increase of fat, corpulence; — enlargement of the scrotum.

ety: for A. معدة měʻdat, or maʻidat, v.n. fr. معد 'to draw or seize rapidly; to carry off'
def: s.m. The stomach; the hypochondrium.

mere mi'de mẽ dard ho rahā hai.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معده (mi'da), from Arabic مِعْدة (miʕda).

def: s.m. = meṅḍak, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A frog; toad; — rump (of a horse)
more: s. m. A frog. See مينڐک menḍak.
subs: s.m. The frog abscess (a painful imposthume which grows on the sole of the foot, and usually terminates in the course of a few days by suppuration).

def: adj. (f. -inī), Corpulent, fat; robust, stout, strong.
s.m. Offering, oblation, sacrifice.
s.m. (?) Gleanings of corn left on the field.

more: s. m. See ميد med, sign. 1.

s.f. Mental vigour or power, understanding, apprehension, intelligence, intellect, conception, sense, prudence, wisdom, sagacity, genius
subs: (˚dhā+at˚), s.m. Name of a Kāṇv (author of some of the hymns of the Rig-veda); — name of the father of Kaṇva; of a son of Manu Svāyambhuva; of one of the seven sages under Manu Sāvarṇ; and of others; — name of a river.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मे॒धा (medhā́).

def: adj. (f. -inī), Possessed of mental power, intelligent, intellectual, wise, judicious, endowed with sound judgment, clever, learned; docile.

s.f. = medhi, or medh, q.v. s.m. A pillar or post in the middle of a threshing-floor (around which the cattle turn in treading out the corn); — the part of a threshing-floor around which cattle turn as they tread out the corn.
adj. = medhāvī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Possessed of mental power, intelligent, intellectual, wise, judicious, endowed with sound judgment, clever, learned; docile.
s.m. A pillar or post in the middle of a threshing-floor (around which the cattle turn in treading out the corn); — the part of a threshing-floor around which cattle turn as they tread out the corn.
adj. = medhāvī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Possessed of mental power, intelligent, intellectual, wise, judicious, endowed with sound judgment, clever, learned; docile.
s.m. A ram (i.q. meṛhā); — the penis.
s.f. The earth; land, soil, ground, country, landed property; — a body of pilgrims going to visit the tomb of a saint.

s. f. 1. The earth. 2. A body of pilgrims going to visit the tomb of a saint.

def: s.m. A cloud; — fog, mist.
more: s. m. 1. A cloud. 2. A mist, a fog.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian میغ (mēġ, “cloud; fog, mist”). Doublet of مین٘ہ (mẽh).

def: s.m. = megh-ḍambar, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) Whit leprosy.

n. m. Pr. mekho or meho a cloud; P. meg/?/ . 1. A cloud. 2. Rain. 3. A rāg or musical mode appropriate to the wet season and last watch of the night before the first dawn of day, said to have proceeded from the head of Brahmā, or the sky. megh-ḍambar , 1. A large high tent. 2. A kind of palanquin. megh-patī , n. m. Lord of the clouds; Indrā. megh-rāj , n. m. The thunderer; Indra. meghā-ghām , n. f. 1. The heat of a cloudy day. 2. Sultriness. megh-nād , n. f. The rumbling of clouds; thunder.
more: s. m. 1. A cloud. 2. Name of a rāg or musical mode appropriated to the wet season and last watch of the night before the first dawn of day, and said to have proceeded from the head of Brahmā, or else from the sky. ميکهـ برن megh-baran ( मेघवर्ण ) adj. Of the colour of the clouds. ميگهپتي मेघपति megh-pati, s. m. 1. Lord of the clouds. 2. A name of Indra. ميگهراج मेघराज megh-rāj, s. m. The thunderer.
wety: From Sanskrit मेघ (megha).

def: s.m. A cloud; — rain (i.q. meṅh); — name of a rāg or musical mode, appropriated to the rainy season, and last watch of the night before the dawn of day (said to have proceeded from the head of Brahmā, or from the sky); — a kind of fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus; — name of a rākshas or demon
subs: (˚gha+āć˚), adj. (f. -ā), Overspread or covered with clouds, shaded by clouds, clouded
(˚ga+āg˚), s.m. 'Approach of clouds,' the rainy season, the rains
(meghā = S. megha+kah̥, or maigha+kah̥), s.f. Heat of a cloudy day; sultriness
or megh-banar, s.m. An epithet of the clouds
vulg. megh-patī, s.m. 'Lord of the cloud,' the thunderer, an epithet of Indra
s.m. 'Cloud-blossom'; water; river-water; hail; — name of one of the four horses of Vishṇu or Krishṇ
s.m. 'Cloud-darkness,' darkness resulting from a clouded sky, cloudiness; cloudy or rainy weather
s.m. Cloud-light,' a flash of lightning
s.m. Thunder; — a mass of clouds; shadow of clouds; — the umbrella of Indra; an umbrella, or a canopy (over an elephant's hauda, or over an idol, &c.); — a large, high tent; — a kind of palanquin
s.m. The musical scale megh, q.v. (it is supposed capable of bringing down rain from heaven)
adj. In the form of clouds; cloud-like; resounding, or thunder-ing; black and threatening
s.m. 'Cloud-time'; the rainy season, the rains
s.f. A line or succession of clouds; a gathering of clouds

s.m. 'Cloud-noise,' rumbling or thundering of clouds; sound of rain; thunder; — an epithet of Varuṇ, and of a son of his, and of a son of Rāvaṇ (i.q. indra-jit); — a species of amaranth, Amaranthus polygonoides; — the palās tree, Butea frondosa
(˚nāda + an˚), s.m. 'Rejoicing in the rumbling of thunder-clouds,' an epithet of the peacock
vulg. megh-baran, or megh-banar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), 'Cloud-coloured,' having the hue of a cloud; dark like the clouds; — a proper name; name of a crow in the Hitopadeś.

def: s.m. A magazine.
s.m. = مينہہ meṅh, q.v. s.m. Rain; a shower
subs: v.n. Rain to come, or to threaten

subs: (˚sha+an˚), s.m. (lit. 'having ram's testicles'), An epithet of Indra
mesh-pālak, or mesh-pālī, s.m. A shepherd
adj. & s.m. Sheep-eyed; sheepish; — one having eyes like a sheep's; (met.) a blockhead

def: s. m. Rain. See مينـﮩ meṅh.
s.m. Discharging urine; — urine; — urinary disease, excessive discharge of urine, diabetes; — inflammation of the urethra; gonorrhœa, clap; gleet.
s.m. (Dakh.) Gonorrhœa, &c. (i.q. meh, q.v.). s.m. = مينہہ meṅh, q.v. s.m. Rain; a shower
subs: v.n. Rain to come, or to threaten
or meṅh paṛnā, v.n. Rain to fall; to rain

s. m. A taunter.

subs: or shutur-ě-be-mahār, adj. Unbridled, unrestrained, loose.

A piece of wood, with a string attached to it, put through the nose of a camel (to guide him by); a camel's nose-string; — reins; a bridle (the word is also used in connection with shutur, 'camel,' as rās, 'rein,' of horses, and 'head,' of cattle)
subs: or shutur-ě-be-mahār, adj. Unbridled, unrestrained, loose.

def: s.m. An embroidered cover; — bed-furniture.

s.f. = مينہدي meṅhdī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Effeminate, womanly; — an effeminate man; a man who appears in the habit of a woman (i.q. mahrā, or měhrā, q.v.).
n. f. S. महिमा 1. Veneration; reverence. 2. Kindness; affability. 3. Glory. mehmā se bolnā , v. n. To speak mildly, gently. mehmā k . To extol; glorify; magnify.
more: s. f. Greatness (in general, literal or figurative).

def: n. m. 1. Aguest. Ek din mehmān, do din mehmān, tīsre din balāë jān . Prov. One day a guest, two days a guest, the third day an unwelcome pest. 2. ( Rus .) A son-in-law. mehmān-khāna, mehmāṅ-sarāë, sarā , n. m. A house for the reception of travellers and strangers. mehmān-dār , n. m. 1. An entertainer; a host. 2. An officer appointed to receive and entertain an ambassador, etc. mehmān-dārī, mehmān-navāzī , n. f. Entertainment; hospitality. mehmān-dārī k. , v. n. To entertain; show hospitality. mehmān-navāz , adj. Hospitable. Irānī mehmāṅ-navāz haiṅ . mehmān'ī ; Illit. mijmānī , n. f. A feast.
more: s. m. f. 1. A stranger. 2. A guest. 3. A son-in-law. مـﮩمان خانه mihmān-ḵẖāna, A house for the reception of a stranger, a dining room. مـﮩمان دار mihmān-dār, 1. An entertainer, host. 2. An officer appointed to receive and entertain an ambassadour. مـﮩمانداري mihmān-dār-ī, s. f. Entertainment, hospitality. مـﮩمان دوست mihmān-dost, Hospitable.

def: s. m. A guest. See مـﮩمان mihmān.
adj. Hospitable
subs: or měhmān-sarāʼe, s.m. A house for the reception or entertainment of travellers and strangers; an inn, a caravansary; a hospitable hall, a dining-room
s.m. A hospitable man; one who has the charge of receiving and entertaining guests; an entertainer, a host; — an officer appointed to receive and entertain an ambassador, &c.
s.f. Entertainment; hospitality, a waiting upon guests
adj. Hospitable; — s.m. A hospitable man (i.q. měhmān-dār)

def: s.m. = مہمان měhmān, q.v.

s.f. Entertainment; hospitality; an entertainment, a feast, a banquet; — adj. Of or pertaining to a guest
subs: (-kī), To give an entertainment (to), to prepare a feast (for), to entertain, to treat hospitably.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِهْمانِی (mihmānī).

subs: s.m. A horse that will bear the spur.

def: s.m. Reviling; quizzing; sarcasm, taunt; fun (i.q. mehnā; — syn. bolī-ṭholī; ṭhesrā)
subs: or měhnā mārnā (-par?), To poke fun (at); to sneer (at); to taunt, to ridicule; to quiz.

def: s.m. Taunt, &c. (i.q. مہنا mehnā, q.v.)
subs: (-par), To taunt, ridicule, make fun (of), quiz, laugh (at).

def: n. f. A sarcasm.
more: s. m. Sarcasm. مـﮩنا پهينکنا mihnā phenknā, To sneer.

def: s. m. A taunt. ميـﮩنا مارنا mehnā mārnā, v. a. To taunt.

ety: for A. محنة, v.n. fr. محن 'to try, prove,' &c.
def: s.f. Labour, moil, toil, drudgery; industry, diligence; application, attention; exercise; athletic exercise; — trial, sore trial, temptation, trouble, affliction, misery, calamity, misfortune; evil, inconvenience, sorrow, teen, perplexity, difficulty
more: n. f. 1. Labor (اُدم 1, 2). 2. Application; attention; diligence. 3. Athletic exercise. mehnat uṭhānā , v. n. To labor; take trouble. mehnat ṭhikāne lagnā , v. n. To turn to account (one's labor). mehnat-i-shāqqa , Hard labor. mehnat-i-shabā-rozī , Working night and day. mehnat k. , v. a. To labor; exert oneself ( uddam k .) mehnat mazdūrī , 1. Labor and wages. 2. Daily labour; job work. dil o jān se kām k. , v. a. To go to work heart and soul.

s. f. (dakh. s. m.) Labour, toil, industry, exercise, trouble, misfortune, difficulty, calamity, affliction, trial, temptation, perplexity, sorrow. محنت آباد miḥnat-ābād, Full of misery. محنت زده miḥnat-zada, or محنت کش miḥnat-kash, adj. Enduring labour or trouble, afflicted. محنت کرنا miḥnat karnā, To labour, to toil. محنت کشي miḥnat-kash-ī, s. f. Endurance of labour or trouble, affliction. محنت کوپينا miḥnat ko pīnā (dakh. i. q. متحمل هونا) To endure, be patient (?).
subs: adj. Full of misery, &c.; — s.m. The abode of sorrow or trouble
v.n. To undergo labour or trouble, &c.; to labour, to take pains
(-kī), Labour (of a person) to turn to account; — měḥnat-zada, adj. & s.m. Distressed, wretched, miserable; — distrest one, miserable creature, poor wretch
Work or toil of night and day
v.n. To labour, or toil (for or after, -ke pīćhe); to exert oneself
adj. Enduring labour or trouble; moiling, drudging; afflicted, distressed; oppressed
s.f. Endurance of labour or trouble, &c.; affliction, misery, suffering
(apnī), To swallow (one's) trouble or sorrow,' to restrain oneself, to bear patiently
s.f. Labour and wages; — daily labour; job-work.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِحْنَت (mihnat), borrowed from Arabic مِحْنَة (miḥna). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi محنت (mḥnt /⁠mihnat⁠/).

def: s.f. corr. of محنت měḥnat, q.v.

ety: měḥnat, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: s.m. Remuneration, hire, wages, fee or fees.
more: n. m. Remuneration; fee. mehnatānah ajūrah , The wages of labor. mehnatānah takhmīnī , Estimated amount of fees. mehnatānah-i-vakīl , Pleader's or Counsel's fee.

ety: rel. n. fr. měḥnat; — ˚ī = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Labouring; laborious, industrious, painstaking; hardy; — difficult; — unfortunate; tried by afflictions; — a labouring man, labourer; — a laborious or industrious person, &c.
more: n. m. mehntan , n. f. One who labors; a laborer.
adj. Laborious, labouring, industrious, difficult, unfortunate; labouring person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian محنتی (mihnatī).

def: s.f. = مينہدي meṅhdī, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit मेन्धिका (mendhikā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A fuzz-ball, a puff-ball.

s.m. = مہرا mahrā or měhrā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A man who appears in the habit of a woman; an effeminate person (see měhlā); — a kahār, or pālkī-bearer (so called because they have access to the women's apartments).

ety: n. of place fr. حرب; see ḥarb
def: s.f. The uppermost or chief sitting-place (in a room), the place where kings and chiefs and great men sit; a high place; a king's closet or private chamber; — the station of the Imām in a mosque, the principal place in a mosque where the priest prays before the people with his face turned towards Mecca; the niche (which shows the direction of the qibla) of a mosque; — a niche; a shelf; an arch
more: n. f. 1. An arch. 2. The principal place in a mosque where the priest prays before the people with his face turned towards Mecca. 3. The royal closet or chamber. mehrāb-i-teg . The curve or arch of a scimitar. mehrāb-dār, mehrābī , adj. Arched. mehrāb-dār barmā . A bow-drill. mehrāb-dār khiṛkī , n. f. A bow-window. kaṭvāṅ mehrāb . A crenated arch. vasat-i-mehrāb . The span of an arch.

(from حرب) s. f. 1. The principal place in a mosque where the priest prays before the people with his face turned towards Mecca. 2. The royal closet or chamber. 3. (In dakh.) Shelf, niche: see طاق t̤āḳ. محراب تيغ miḥrāb-i-teg̠ẖ, The curve or arch of a scimitar (as, the محراب is always arched).
subs: s.f. The curve or arch of a scimitar
adj. Having a měḥrāb; arched; bowed, bow (as a window, or a drill, &c.)
s.f. The span of an arch.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian محراب (mihrāb), from Arabic مِحْرَاب (miḥrāb).

def: adj. Having a měḥrāb; of or like a měḥrāb; arched, &c. ( = měḥrāb-dār, q.v.).
more: adj. Of or relating to the miḥrāb, arched.

def: s.f. Woman, female; wife.

Sant. n. f. S. महिला A woman ( istrī ). 'Adrā meṅ jo satuā khāë, bin mehrī sasurārī jāë, Jeṭh Baisākh jo chaṛhe pauā, kahe, Ghāgh, ī tīnoṅ kauā' 'Bhūkā ho to satuā khāë, prīti base sasurārī jāë, Rasiā marad ho chaṛhe pauā, kahe bahuriā Ghāghe kauā .' 'Who in the rainy season eats gram meal, Wifeless who to his father-in-law's doth steal, In May and June who to his wife's bed goes, These three, says Ghāg, are all so many crows. 'Whom hunger gnaws may surely gram-meal eat, Love to the father-in-law's may guide one's feet, The wanton warm may to his wife's bed go, The daughter-in-law says, Ghāg himself's a crow.'

adj. Friendly; kind; gracious. Khudā mehrbān ho to kul mehrbān . Prov. When God is gracious the world is gracious. mehrbān , n. m. A friend ( dost , 7). mehrbān h. , v. n. 1. To be kind, gracious, favorable to. 2. To pay a visit to. Mātā rānī mehr-vān huī hai . Wom.

n. f. Kindness; favor. 1. Āp kī mehrbānī hai! Thank you! 2. Dil se lutf o mehrbānī aur hai, Mehrbānī kī nishānī aur hai . Mohsin. A different thing is kindness from the heart, Its touching tokens are a thing apart. mehrbānī k. yā mehrbānī se pesh ānā , v. a. To be kind to; to confer a favor on.
more: s. f. (dakh. s. m.) Friendliness, kindness, favour. مـﮩرباني کرني mihrbān-ī karnī, To favour, to confer favour (on), to befriend.

ety: Prk. & S. महिला, with w inserted
def: s.f. Woman (i.q. mahar; měhrārū).
adj. Greater (compar. of měh, q.v.); — s.m. A prince; a chief or principal person, headman; — an honorific title of sweepers, inn-keepers, shoe-makers, butchers, &c.; — a sweeper.
n. m. mehtrānī , n. f. 1. A head or chief man. 2. An honorific title of sweepers, inn-keepers, shoe-makers, etc.
more: 1. adj. (compar. of مه mih. Sans. महत्तर ) Greater. 2. s. m. A prince. 3. A sweeper.

def: s.m. = H ميہتراني मेहतरानी mehtrānī, s.f. corr. of مہتر měhtar, and مہتراني měhtrānī, qq.v.
s.f. Chiefship, headship; — perquisites of a chief of the měhtar or scavenger class.
s.f. A woman of the měhtar caste; — the wife of a sweeper or scavenger; a sweeper-woman; — a female inn-keeper.
s.f. = H ميہتر मेहतर mehtar, s.m. corr. of مہتر měhtar, and مہتراني měhtrānī, qq.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = مينہہ meṅh, q.v. s.m. Rain; a shower
subs: v.n. Rain to come, or to threaten
or meṅh paṛnā, v.n. Rain to fall; to rain

ety: v.n., or n. of instr., fr. حور 'to return; to turn round,' &c.
def: s.m. Axis (of a pulley, &c.); an axletree (of wood or iron); the pin on which the tongue of a buckle turns
more: n. m. An axis. mehvar-i-zamīn , The axis of the earth.
(noun of instrument from حور) s. m. An axis.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِحْوَر (miḥwar).

def: s.f. corr. of ميز mez, q.v. s.f. A table
subs: or mez-mān, s.m. Landlord; master of the house, or of a feast; entertainer, host
To lay the table, or the table-cloth

def: s.m. Major.
s.m. (dialec.) A ram.
s.m. Magistrate (see majisṭreṭ).
s.m. Name of a mountain (in which the river Narmadā rises; cf. amar-kanṭak)
subs: or mekal-kanyakā, or mekal-sutā, s.f. 'Daughter of Mekala,' an epithet of the Narmadā river (also mekhala-kanyakā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of megh-ḍambar, q.v.

ety: for mīḵẖ; old P. mikh; Pehl. miś, or maś; Zend mizhda
def: s.f. A nail; a pin (of wood or iron); peg, tent-peg; stake, pile; post; wedge; spike; hook, tenter
more: n. f. A nail; peg; stake; tentpeg; spike. mekh ṭhoṅknā , v. a. 1. To drive a stake into the hands and feet. (An oriental punishment). 2. To impale; crucify. mekh-chū , n. f. A mallet; hammer. mekh mārnā , To put a spoke in the wheel ( bhāṅjī mārnā ). mekhī rupayā , n. m. A rupee which has been hollowed and filled with lead, copper, etc. bad ghoṛe kī mekh , Fac. A dwarfish woman. The simile is taken from the well-driven stake to which a vicious horse is tethered, a small portion only being above ground.
s. f. A nail, a wedge, a tent-pin, peg, a hook, a tenter. ميخ ٿهونکنا meḵẖ ṭhonknā, To nail. ميخ چو meḵẖ-chū, s. f. A mallet, a mall, a hammer. ميخ مارنا meḵẖ mārnā, To overcome.
subs: (-meṅ), To hammer or drive a nail, or pin, &c. (into); to drive stakes (into the hands and feet, — an oriental mode of punishment); to impale, to crucify
or meḵẖ-ćū (˚ćo or ćū prob. contrac. of ćob or ćūb), s.f. A mallet, mall, or hammer (for driving piles, pegs, or tent-pins)
(-meṅ), To drive a nail, &c. (into); to put a spoke (in the wheel of); to render powerless, to overcome.

def: s.m. A ram, &c. (i.q. mesh, q.v.). s.m. A ram; sheep; — the sign Aries; a kind of drug
subs: (˚sha+an˚), s.m. (lit. 'having ram's testicles'), An epithet of Indra
mesh-pālak, or mesh-pālī, s.m. A shepherd
adj. & s.m. Sheep-eyed; sheepish; — one having eyes like a sheep's; (met.) a blockhead

def: n. m. S. मेष The sign Aries (A. hamal ).
more: s. m. 1. A ram. 2. The sign Aries. See ميش mesh.

def: s.f. corr. of ميخ meḵẖ, q.v. s.f. A nail; a pin (of wood or iron); peg, tent-peg; stake, pile; post; wedge; spike; hook, tenter
subs: (-meṅ), To hammer or drive a nail, or pin, &c. (into); to drive stakes (into the hands and feet, — an oriental mode of punishment); to impale, to crucify
or meḵẖ-ćū (˚ćo or ćū prob. contrac. of ćob or ćūb), s.f. A mallet, mall, or hammer (for driving piles, pegs, or tent-pins)
(-meṅ), To drive a nail, &c. (into); to put a spoke (in the wheel of); to render powerless, to overcome.

def: s.f. (in one or two compounds, mekhala), A girdle, zone, belt, waist-belt; the zone of a woman; — the triple zone or string worn round the loins by the first three classes of Hindūs (that of a Brāhmaṇ ought to be of munj, that of a Kshatriya of mūrvā, and that of a Vaiśya of san or hemp); — the sacrificial string of a Brāhmaṇ (when made of deer-skin); — girth (of a horse); — a band, or fillet; — a sword-belt, baldric; — a sword-knot, or string fastened to the hilt; — the place of the girdle, the hips; — the cords or lines drawn round an altar, or on the four sides of the hole in which sacrificial fire is offered; — the edge or slope of a mountain; — the sea (as surrounding the land); — name of the Narmadā river
s.m. (f. -inī), One who wears a girdle or zone (mekhlā); a Brāhmaṇical student or youth who, previous to his marriage, wears a girdle suited to his caste; a Brahma-ćārī.
s.m. (dialec.) A frog.
adj. Pierced, or bored with a meḵẖ or iron pin; plugged
subs: s.m. A rupee which has been hollowed, and then plugged with lead, or copper, or the like.

def: A rupee which has been hollowed and then filled with lead, copper, &c. adulterated.
s.f. Sackcloth.
more: (perhaps, from मेष A sheep; q. d. Made of wool) s. f. A sackcloth.

def: s.m. Meeting, assemblage, company; a large concourse of people (for religious or commercial purposes), a fair; a meeting, or mixed assemblage, of faqīrs
more: n. m. S. मेलक 1. Meeting; company. 1. Ek akelā, do kā melā . Prov. Saying. Alone you are lonely, two are company. 2. Jite jī kā melā hai . Prov. While life lasts the fair lasts. 3. Melā ( merā ) dekh āë? Double entendre. Have you seen the fair (my tool)? 4. Bele ke hār haiṅ shauqin albele ko! Pahan le, chalā jā Farangi Mahal ke mele ko! Lucknow street cry . Of jessamine a garland buy, meet for the saucy fair! Wear it about your neck and to the Frank's great fair repair! 5. Mele meṅ jhamelā huā hī kartā hai . Prov. A fair is a crush. 2. A fair. 3. A meeting of faqīrs . melā juṛnā yā lagnā , v. n. To be assembled (a fair). melā k. , v. a. 1. To go to a fair. 2. To convene co-religionists for religious ceremonies or obsequies.

s. m. A fair, a concourse of people for religious or commercial purposes, a mixed assemblage of faḳīrs. ميلا ملنا melā milnā, To meet full in the face. ميلا ٿهيلا melā ṭhelā, s. m. A crowd, a concourse of people.
subs: s.m. A crowding and pushing; — a large concourse of people, a crowd, throng
or melā lagnā, A large concourse of people to be or become assembled; a fair to be held
(-kā), To hold a fair; — to convene co-religionists for religious ceremonies or obsequies; — to go to a fair.

def: s.m. Meeting, coming together, assemblage; union, intercourse; mixture, mixing together; connexion, relation, relationship; association, combination; reconciliation; agreement, compact; concord, intimacy, amity; harmonious consistency together; — balancing (as of an account), tally; — counterpart; — kind, sort; — a band, or company; a court (of justice)
subs: or mel-milāp, s.m. Association, combination; friendly intercourse; familiarity; — reconcilement
adj. (f. -kī), Of the same kind, or class, or society; — congenial; friendly, sociable, &c. (i.q. melī, q.v.)
(-se, or -ke sāth), To unite, or combine (with); to mix, or associate (with), &c. (i.q. milāʼo karnā, and ittifāq karnā, qq.v.)
(-se), To unite, or mix (with); to pair (with); to accord, or to be in harmony (with)
s.m. Close intimacy, or alliance; — familiarity, &c. (i.q. mel); — hel-mel karnā (-se, or -ke sāth), To form an intimacy, or an alliance (with), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

def: s.m. A meeting, coming together, an assembling, associating; union, intercourse.

ety: mel(nā), q.v.+ān = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.m. (dialec.) The herding and feeding of cattle at night.
more: (or mayalān, from ميل) s. m. 1. Inclination, affection, respect, regard. 2. Loving.

def: s.m. Meeting, coming together, assembling; union, junction; associating (with); an encounter; — mixing (with), mixture; — adding (to).
v.t. 1˚ (Mārwārī) To send, &c. (i.q. melnā); — 2˚ (Braj) = melnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -inī) & s.m. Partaking (in); friendly, sociable, familiar; — congenial; of the same kind, or class, or society; — a partaker (in); a friendly or sociable person, &c.
more: 1. s. m. A partaker. 2. adj. Friendly, sociable.

ety: mel˚ = Prk. मेल(इ), or मेले(इ) = S. मेलय(ति), caus. of rt. मिल्; see milnā
def: v.t. To cause to meet, or come together; to congregate together, to convene; — to halt (an army, &c.); — to put (in), to mix (with); to thrust (in or into), cram (in), force (in); to penetrate; — (dialec.) to send, send forth; to give forth, to utter (a cry); to cry to, to call (cf. melwo).
more: v. a. S. मिल् to mix. See دَالنا 1. To put in; mix with. 2. To penetrate; force in; stuff; cram ( ḍālnā 7). 3. (Mar.) To send. 4. To cry; call. Kājī ho-ke bāṅg mele, jo kyā Sāhab bahrā hai? Kabīr. Kīṛe ke pag. nīpur bāje, so bhī Sāhab suntā hai . You Qāzi calls aloud to prayer, Is God then deaf, doth he not hear? No ant that creeps along the ground But in His ear's a bell-like sound.
1. v. a. (perhaps, for पेलन ) To thrust in, stuff in, cram, penetrate, force in. 2. v. n. See ملنا milnā.

def: v.t. (Braj) = melnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to meet, or come together; to congregate together, to convene; — to halt (an army, &c.); — to put (in), to mix (with); to thrust (in or into), cram (in), force (in); to penetrate; — (dialec.) to send, send forth; to give forth, to utter (a cry); to cry to, to call (cf. melwo).
s.f. Madam, maʼam; lady (com. applied to married European ladies)
more: n. f. Cor. of Eng. maam , madam.
subs: or vulg. mem-ṣāḥib, s.f. = mem (by some of the ignorant the term mem-ṣāḥiba is applied to Her Majesty the Queen, whom they regard as the wife of the lāṭ-ṣāḥib or Governor-General).

ety: n. of instr. fr. عمر 'to build,' &c.
def: s.m. A builder, an architect, a mason.
more: n. m. عمر A mason; architect ( rāj , 1).
(from عمر Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A builder, architect, mason. (Pers. plur. معماران).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِعْمَار (miʕmār).

def: s.f. Building; architecture; masonry; — the occupation of a builder, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Building; masonry; architecture. 2. The occupation of a mason.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِعْمَارِی (mi'mārī), from مِعْمَار (mi'mār, “architect”) + ـِی (-ī, suffix forming abstract nouns). By surface analysis, مِعْمار (mi'mār) + ـِی (-ī).

ety: S. मे (onomat.) redupl.; A. also měʼ-měʼ (onomat.)
def: s.f. Bleating, bleat (of a lamb, or kid); — prattle, babble, nonsensical talk.

ety: mem˚ = mema = S. मे (onomat.) redupl.+nā = lā = S. ल+कः; cf. me-me, and mimiyānā
def: s.m. A kid.

ety: Ap. Prk. मज्झहिं; Prk. मझि, मज्झि, मज्झे; S. मध्ये, loc. of मध्य
def: postpn. (of the loc. case), In, into, in the midst of, within, among, between; at; with; to; as regards, concerning; on account of
more: postpos. In, among, between, at. مين سي meṅ se, From within, out of. ¶ maiṅ (and, rarely, meṅ. Sans. अहं, मे &c.) pron. I. مين پنا main-panā, s. m. (dakh.) Egotism, arrogance. مين خوش ميرا خدا خوش maiṅ ḵẖụsh merā ḵẖudā ḵẖụsh, is used to express high satisfaction with any circumstance or proposition. گر ناخوشي سي ميري هوتاهي جي تراخوش جس مين تري خوشي هو مين خوش مرا خدا خوش Gar nā-ḵẖụshī se merī hotā hai jī tirā ḵẖụsh; jis meṅ tirī ḵẖụshī ho maiṅ ḵẖu̇sh mirā ḵẖudā ḵẖụsh. If you are pleased with that which gives me pain; I am delighted and my God is pleased with whatever affords gratification to you.
subs: postpn. From within; from among; out of.

def: postpos. S. मध्ये In; within; at; between; among. meṅ se ; Sant. khon . From within; out of.
more: pron. S. मया Pr. me, maë by me. I. 1. Maiṅ kī gardan par chhurī . Prov. Saying. On my (the kid's) throat a knife. 2. Maiṅ maiṅ chhāṛ, bāore, phirtā māyā meṅ lipṭānā re! Bahū parivār dekh mat bhūle, saṅg na koī jānā re! Kabir. Self, fool, cast off, all wrapt up in delusive wiles of sense! Be not puffed up by kith and kin, none with you 'll go from hence. maiṅ maiṅ . Egotism ( ahaṅkār ). maiṅ maiṅ k. , v. n. To be full of oneself.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa मइं (maĩ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀏 (mae), from Sanskrit मया (mayā).

def: n. f. The bleating of a goat. meṅ meṅ k. , v. a. To bleat.
s.m. (dialec.) A kind of palanquin (with curtains).
s.f. Name of an Apsaras of Indra's heaven (wife of Hima-vat and mother of Pārvatī; — i.q. menakā).
s.f. Name of an Apsaras (wife of Hima-vat, and mother of Pārvatī; — i.q. menā).

s.f. = مينڙ meṅṛ, q.v. s.f. A limit, limitary dike or bank; boundary-mound (of a field); a bank, an embankment; a dam, dike, mole, mound; a landmark; border, edge; hedge, fence; — a high wave, billow, surge (cf. meṅḍhā)
more: s. f. A bank (raised to separate fields), a border, bank (of a pond, river, &c.), dam, dike ( Harris ). مينڐ پڙنا menḍ paṛnā, The rising of waves in the sea.
subs: s.f. A fixing or settling of boundaries; a record of boundaries

def: n. f. 1. A boundary mound (of a field); land-mark; a dam. Eklo maiṅ kahāṅ jāūṅ, chanā kāṭūṅ yā meṅḍ banāūṅ? Alone which way am I to go? Or cut the gram-crop, or the dam build up must I? 2. High waves; billows; surge. meṅḍ-bandī , n. f. A record of boundaries. meṅḍ paṛnā . The rising of waves. meṅḍiyā'nā , v. n. To make a bank or dam.
s.f. = meṅṛā, q.v. s.f. The brim, or parapet, of a well.
more: s. f. The brim of a well.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A he-goat (with curling horns).

n. m. meṅḍkī , n. f. S. मण्डूक Pālī, meṅḍuko , Pr. manḍuro . 1. A frog; toad. 1. Sarkanḍoṅ kī gāṛī banāī, meṅḍak jote jāëṅ, Rājā mārī podnī to bair bisāvan jāëṅ . Boy's rhyme. The little bird built a chariot of reeds, and one pair of frogs put to, And valiantly went to fight with the king who had slain his own mate true. 2. Menḍkī ko bhī zukām huā . Prov. Even the frog has a cold. (What airs he gives himself!) 3. Isī pānī meṅ meṅḍak, machhlī yā pānī meṅ biyāpe, Yā pānī kī ṭhākur pūjā, sod kahāṅ se āī? Kabīr. In this water frogs and fishes have their birth and live, How then call this holy water which your gods you give. 4. Bībī meṅṛkī, tu to pānī meṅ kīrānī, Kavve tere bhāī, bhatīje, chil terī deorānī! Girls' song. O lady frog! you are the water's queen. The crows your brothers, nephews are, I ween, Wife of your younger brother kite unclean. 2. ( meṅḍkī ) The rump of a horse. 3. A toy. meṅḍak phoṛā , The frog-abscess. A painful imposthume which grows on the sole of the foot and usually terminates in the course of a few days by suppuration.
s.m. (f. -ī), A frog; toad; — rump (of a horse)
more: s. m. ( मंडूक ) A frog; the rump of a horse. مينڐکي کو زکام هونا menḍkī ko zukām honā (lit. A frog having caught cold) expresses pride or consequential airs assumed by worthless persons.
subs: s.m. The frog abscess (a painful imposthume which grows on the sole of the foot, and usually terminates in the course of a few days by suppuration).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit मंडुक्क (maṃḍukka), from Sanskrit म॒ण्डूक॑ (maṇḍū́ka).

ety: meṅḍak, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A frog; toad
more: s. f. ( मंडूक ) A frog; the rump of a horse. مينڐکي کو زکام هونا menḍkī ko zukām honā (lit. A frog having caught cold) expresses pride or consequential airs assumed by worthless persons.
subs: 'Has the frog caught cold?'; an insignificant, or worthless, fellow (like you) giving yourself airs of importance?

ety: Prk. मेंढओ; S. मेण्ढकः, fr. मेढ्रकः
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A ram; — a large or high wave (cf. meṅṛ); the swell of the tide called the boar.
more: s. m. 1. A ram. 2. The swell of the tide called the boar.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑁂𑀁𑀠 (meṃḍha), from Sanskrit मेण्ढ (meṇḍha), from Munda.

def: s.m. The fever-nut, the fruit of Guilandina bonducella (syn. kaṭkaranj); — a kind of fire-work, made of this nut, which bursts with an explosion.
more: s. A fruit (Guilandina bonduccella: Lin. ) so called, filled with composition (as a firework); when lighted it is thrown amidst the crowd. It bursts with an explosion. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.f. = meṅhdī, q.v.
s.f. (colloq.) corr. of mendhī, or meṅhdī, q.v.
s.f. A she-goat (with curling horns).
s.f. (dialec.) = bīṅḍī, q.v. s.f. Dot over a letter; dot, point, spot, mark, cypher, nought; — spangle, ornament for the forehead.
Mah. n. f. S. बेणी See bīṅḍī .

ety: meṅḍiy˚ = meṅḍī = meṅḍ, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwa or āwe = S. āpi, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To skirt, or inclose, with a bank or fence; to make a bank, or dike, or dam, round (a place).
more: (from مينڐ) v. n. To skirt, to enclose with a bank.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = main-phal, q.v. s.m. See s.v. main. s.f. Maternal uncle's wife, aunt ( = māmī); — a respectful term of address to elderly women (prob. for māʼī).
